Depending on the configuration of MTU
1. Ethertype Indicates protocols such as 802.3, 802.3an, etc.
The payload field is the MTU, which controls the size of the dat
a carried by a TCP/UDP message. data exceeding the MTU needs
to be fragmented into multiple messages for transmission, and t
hen assembled and merged at the receiving end. the size of the
MTU has a direct impact on the efficiency and performance of ne
twork transmission. A smaller MTU results in more frequent frag
mentation and more network overhead, while a larger MTU redu
ces network overhead but may increase the risk of fragmentation
and the likelihood of transmission failure.
1. The protocol field indicates TCP or UDP
2. IP base message length is 20 bytes
The Internet Header Length field specifies
the length of the IP header in 4 bytes. It
indicates the size of the header and allows for
The IHL value is multiplied by 4 to determine
4. The Total Length field represents the total
size of the IP packet, including both the
header and the data payload. It specifies the
entire length in bytes, starting from the IP
header to the end of the packet.
The length field in TCP and UDP is the message length, and
the size of the data it carries is controlled by the MTU.
2. TCP base message length is 20 bytes
3. UDP base message length is 8 bytes
